ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Water Galura / GOLD FEATHER) GPO-pushed Chrome credential harvester (IpScan.bat / IpScan.ps1)
ucs.description: Hunts the highly distinctive Qilin affiliate tradecraft documented by Sophos X-Ops in 2024: after compromising a domain controller, the operator creates a logon-script GPO that pushes 'IpScan.bat' which invokes 'IpScan.ps1' to copy Chrome 'Login Data' SQLite databases and 'Local State' files from every endpoint to a SYSVOL/share before deploying the Qilin payload. This pre-encryption mass credential theft is the hallmark of Water Galura's double-extortion playbook and is rarely seen outside Qilin intrusions.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_process values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","explorer.exe","svchost.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")) AND (Processes.process IN ("*IpScan.bat*","*IpScan.ps1*","*Login Data*","*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Local State*","*\\Google\\Chrome\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data*","*Edge\\User Data\\Default\\Login Data*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\SYSVOL\\*\\scripts\\IpScan*","*\\NETLOGON\\IpScan*") OR Filesystem.file_name IN ("IpScan.bat","IpScan.ps1","LD","LS") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspiciousNames = dynamic(["IpScan.bat","IpScan.ps1","LD","LS"]);
let chromeArtifacts = dynamic([@"\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Login Data", @"\Google\Chrome\User Data\Local State", @"\Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\Login Data"]);
let gpoExec = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("gpscript.exe","userinit.exe","svchost.exe","explorer.exe")
  | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (suspiciousNames)
     or ProcessCommandLine has_any (chromeArtifacts)
     or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("Copy-Item", "Login Data")
     or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\\(SYSVOL|NETLOGON)\\.*IpScan";
let sysvolDrops = DeviceFileEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
  | where FileName in~ (suspiciousNames)
     or FolderPath has_any (@"\SYSVOL\", @"\NETLOGON\") and FileName matches regex @"(?i)IpScan\.(bat|ps1)$"
  | project Timestamp, DeviceName, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName;
union gpoExec, sysvolDrops
| summarize EventCount=count(), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp),
            CmdLines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 10), DropPaths=make_set(FolderPath, 10),
            Hosts=make_set(DeviceName, 25) by InitiatingProcessAccountName
| where EventCount > 1 or array_length(Hosts) >= 2
ucs.rationale: The 'IpScan.bat' / 'IpScan.ps1' GPO logon-script pattern targeting Chrome 'Login Data' and 'Local State' across an entire domain is a Qilin-specific staging routine first publicly documented by Sophos in August 2024 and has been re-observed in subsequent Water Galura affiliate intrusions. Generic ransomware crews (LockBit, Akira, Play) do not push credential harvesters via SYSVOL logon scripts in

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Water Galura) ESXi/Windows pre-encryption: shadow-copy wipe + bulk VM termination via esxcli
ucs.description: Detects the staged pre-encryption sequence used by Qilin's Rust/Go cross-platform locker: on Windows hosts the operator deletes VSS shadow copies via wmic/vssadmin and disables recovery, and on ESXi hosts (or via SSH from a jump box) issues 'esxcli vm process kill' loops to forcibly terminate every running VM before the Linux Qilin binary encrypts the datastores. This chained Windows-then-ESXi destruction signature is characteristic of Water Galura affiliate engagements against virtualised enterprise estates.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process IN ("*vssadmin*delete*shadows*","*wmic*shadowcopy*delete*","*bcdedit*recoveryenabled*No*","*bcdedit*bootstatuspolicy*ignoreallfailures*","*wbadmin*delete*catalog*","*esxcli*vm*process*kill*","*esxcli*vm*process*list*","*/sbin/vmdumper*","*/bin/vim-cmd*vmsvc/power.off*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process_id _time span=10m | stats dc(process_name) as distinct_destructive_cmds values(cmdline) as cmds values(parent) as parents min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by dest user | where distinct_destructive_cmds >= 2 | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=22 AND All_Traffic.dest_category="esxi" by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_port _time span=10m | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`]
ucs.kql:
let WindowsDestructive = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where (FileName in~ ("vssadmin.exe","wmic.exe","wbadmin.exe","bcdedit.exe","powershell.exe","cmd.exe"))
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("shadowcopy delete","delete shadows /all","recoveryenabled No","bootstatuspolicy ignoreallfailures","delete catalog","Get-WmiObject Win32_Shadowcopy","Remove-WmiObject")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, Indicator="VSS_WIPE";
let EsxiKill = DeviceProcessEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where ProcessCommandLine has_all ("esxcli","vm process kill")
     or ProcessCommandLine has_all ("vim-cmd","vmsvc/power.off")
     or ProcessCommandLine has "/sbin/vmdumper"
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, Indicator="ESXI_KILL";
let SshToEsxi = DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
  | where RemotePort == 22 and ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
  | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("plink.exe","ssh.exe","putty.exe","powershell.exe","openssh.exe")
  | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName=InitiatingProcessAccountName, FileName=InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine=InitiatingProcessCommandLine, Indicator=strcat("SSH_ESXI_", RemoteIP);
union WindowsDestructive, EsxiKill, SshToEsxi
| summarize Indicators=make_set(Indicator), Cmdlines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 20),
            Hosts=make_set(DeviceName, 10), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp),
            EventCount=count() by AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 30m)
| where array_length(Indicators) >= 2
| extend QilinPreEncryptScore = array_length(Indicators) + iff(Indicators has "ESXI_KILL", 2, 0)
| sort by QilinPreEncryptScore desc, LastSeen desc
ucs.rationale: Qilin's locker ships in matched Windows (PE) and Linux/ESXi (ELF) variants — Water Galura affiliates routinely chain a Windows shadow-copy wipe with an ESXi VM-kill loop within the same intrusion window because their RaaS playbook explicitly instructs affiliates to dismount virtual workloads before encrypting datastores. Single-signal vssadmin-delete events fire for many ransomware families, but t
